project_name: MultiPLHY — Sunfire SOEC at Neste Rotterdam
status_detail: World's largest high-temperature steam electrolysis (SOEC) plant commissioned October 2025 at Neste's Rotterdam refinery. 2.6 MW Sunfire SOEC system. Produces renewable hydrogen using waste heat from Neste's renewable diesel production, achieving significantly higher efficiency than conventional low-temperature electrolysis. Project co-funded by EU Horizon 2020 (MultiPLHY consortium).

data_management.narrative_summary: MultiPLHY is the world's largest high-temperature steam electrolysis (SOEC) system, a 2.6 MW unit installed at Neste's Rotterdam refinery and commissioned in October 2025. Developed by Sunfire GmbH and the MultiPLHY consortium (co-funded by EU Horizon 2020), the SOEC system produces renewable hydrogen from steam using waste heat from Neste's renewable diesel production, achieving electrical efficiency of over 84% (LHV) — significantly higher than PEM (55-60%) or Alkaline (62%) electrolysis. The hydrogen is used to replace fossil-derived hydrogen in renewable fuel production, supporting Neste's goal of making its Rotterdam refinery the world's most sustainable refinery. This project demonstrates SOEC technology at pre-commercial scale in an industrial environment.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Neste Rotterdam refinery. Waste heat from renewable diesel production used for steam generation.

offtake.offtaker_name: Neste Rotterdam Refinery (captive use in renewable fuel production)

sources.title: Sunfire commissions world's largest high-temperature electrolyzer at Neste Rotterdam — Oct 2025
sources.author: Sunfire GmbH / Neste
